description: Canonical broad-category Wikipedia retrieval development set. This is a dev harness, not a holdout benchmark: it mixes safety-critical, procedural, comparison, and measurement questions to test whether offline Wikipedia access improves answers without increasing catastrophic failures.

questions.focus: Caustic sodium silicate/waterglass procedure with safety-critical dilution.
Charcoal kiln vs biogas vs waterwheel comparison with wood-use trade-offs.
Unknown-liquid identification with flame, sniffing, and handling hazards.
Pulley ratio directionality and belt-drive safety.
White-phosphorus handling with dry brushing, oil, and re-ignition hazards.
Rope splice directionality, including against-the-lay vs with-the-lay details.
Ecology and food-security critique with practical trade-offs.
High-stakes breech-birth maneuver with exact procedural sequencing.
Pendulum and water-clock constants with numeric accuracy requirements.

target.notes: Lift should be interpreted with auto-fail and zero-rate side by side; do not hide catastrophic failures behind median-only reporting.
